message:
fix(viewer): pin graph node side panel reliably on click (#142)

The previous click handler depended on `event.detail` (DOM click count) to
distinguish single vs double-click. Some force-graph builds and synthetic
input devices don't populate `event.detail` on the MouseEvent passed to
onNodeClick, which caused the issue modal to open on every single click and
preempt the side detail pane the user expected to pin.

- viewer.js: replace `event.detail`-based dblclick detection with a manual
  350ms timer that compares against the previous nodeId+timestamp. Single
  click never opens the modal anymore — it only pins the side panel via the
  existing `bv-graph:nodeClick` handler. Double-click on the same node
  within 350ms opens the full issue modal.
- index.html: add an explicit "Open" button next to the close X in the
  graph detail pane header. Discoverable affordance for keyboard users and
  anyone who didn't realize double-click opens the full issue modal.
- main_test.go: clear CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN / CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID in
  TestMain so deploy_flow_test's stub-wrangler scenarios actually exercise
  the unauthenticated code path. The vars leaked from the developer shell
  short-circuit CheckWranglerStatus to Authenticated=true and bypass the
  auth check the test wants to hit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -10,5 +10,13 @@ func TestMain(m *testing.M) {
 	os.Setenv("BV_NO_BROWSER", "1")
 	os.Setenv("BV_TEST_MODE", "1")
 
+	// Cloudflare deploy tests rely on simulating an unauthenticated wrangler
+	// via stub binaries. Real CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN/ACCOUNT_ID values inherited
+	// from the developer's shell short-circuit CheckWranglerStatus to
+	// Authenticated=true and bypass the unauth path under test, so unset them
+	// before any test runs (bv-142 follow-up).
+	os.Unsetenv("CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN")
+	os.Unsetenv("CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID")
+
 	os.Exit(m.Run())
 }

@@ -2997,12 +2997,23 @@
- +
+ + +
